title: Fix CSWSH risk in shared session :api pipeline
prompt:
The router below has two pipelines that share the same session cookie. The `:api` pipeline handles POST requests but omits `:protect_from_forgery`, creating a cross-site WebSocket hijacking (CSWSH) risk:

```elixir
pipeline :browser do
  plug :accepts, ["html"]
  plug :fetch_session
  plug :protect_from_forgery
end

pipeline :api do
  plug :accepts, ["json"]
  plug :fetch_session
end

scope "/api", MyAppWeb do
  pipe_through :api
  post "/posts/:id/delete", PostController, :delete
end
```

Either: (a) add `:protect_from_forgery` to `:api`, OR (b) use a different authentication mechanism for the API (Bearer token) and do NOT fetch the session cookie at all. Implement option (a) — add `:protect_from_forgery` to the `:api` pipeline. Write the fix to `lib/my_app_web/router.ex`.
tier_rationale: Medium — requires understanding CSWSH and the shared-cookie risk.
research_citation: Phoenix LiveView security docs: 'A cross-site WebSocket hijacking attack is very similar to a CSRF attack... Phoenix applies CSRF protections when the socket is configured to expose the session.'
